result:
If I could change just one thing, I would give every child on Earth—regardless of birthplace, gender, or family income—reliable, safe access to high-quality education up through at least lower-secondary school.  

Education doesn’t merely boost individual earning power; it’s the closest thing humanity has to a universal catalyst. Each additional year of schooling lowers infant mortality, raises crop yields, slows population growth, reduces violent conflict, and speeds up the diffusion of green technologies. A single intervention ripples outward, compounding across health, prosperity, and even climate resilience. And unlike many global fixes, the infrastructure—teachers, books, digital tablets—can be scaled with existing tools once the political will and modest funding are in place.

In short: guarantee every twelve-year-old can read, write, reason, and log on to the internet, and you’ve handed the next generation the keys to solve everything else.

prompt: If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
